segments.caption: The video opens with an aerial view of a vast, rural landscape with fields, rivers, and mountains, transitioning into a prison corridor labeled 'CELDA' (cells) where a man in a red 'LES BELLAMINS B.S.P.H.C UNDERGROUND' t-shirt walks with guards. He gestures toward rows of heavily tattooed inmates in white uniforms seated on the floor, surrounded by masked officers. The scene cuts to a cityscape with a prominent volcano, then to a news montage showing Nayib Bukele’s 2019 presidential victory, including his swearing-in ceremony and public speeches. Subtitles in Arabic appear during his speech, emphasizing his anti-corruption stance and commitment to public safety. The video then shows Bukele addressing a crowd with soldiers in formation, followed by a nighttime prison scene with inmates being led by officers in riot gear. The segment ends with Bukele speaking at a podium with El Salvador flags, with Arabic subtitles translating his remarks about parliamentary criminals and the need to guarantee public safety.

discovered_new_entities.Tattooed inmates: Multiple inmates with extensive tattoos on their heads and bodies, wearing white uniforms and sitting on the floor in a prison corridor, surrounded by guards.
discovered_new_entities.Masked prison guards: Uniformed officers in blue or black uniforms, some wearing balaclavas, standing or patrolling the prison corridor and escorting inmates.
discovered_new_entities.Nayib Bukele: The President of El Salvador, shown in formal attire during his swearing-in ceremony, public speeches, and addressing crowds, with Arabic subtitles translating his statements about corruption and public safety.
discovered_new_entities.El Salvador cityscape: An aerial view of a densely built city with greenery and a prominent volcano in the background, likely San Salvador.
discovered_new_entities.News montage: A compilation of news clips from outlets like The New York Times and The Guardian, showing Bukele’s 2019 election victory and inauguration.
discovered_new_entities.Soldiers in formation: Uniformed military personnel in helmets and tactical gear standing in rows during a public address by Bukele.
discovered_new_entities.Prison exterior at night: A dimly lit prison yard with inmates being led by officers in riot gear, marked with 'DGCP' on their shields.

segments.caption: The video opens with a news clip of Nayib Bukele, President of El Salvador, speaking at a podium with the Salvadoran flag behind him, emphasizing his commitment to public safety, as Arabic subtitles translate his words. The scene transitions to an aerial view of a large, modern prison complex surrounded by high walls and barbed wire, identified by signage as 'CECOT - Centro de Confinamiento del Terrorismo'. A man in a red t-shirt, later identified as the 'Man in red t-shirt', stands outside the prison, gesturing expansively toward the facility while speaking. The camera captures various angles of the prison exterior, including a guard standing near the entrance and a vehicle arriving. The video then shifts to a first-person perspective from inside a van driving along a tree-lined road, with the 'Man in red t-shirt' visible in the back seat, engaging in conversation and gesturing. The journey continues through rural landscapes and into a cityscape with a prominent volcano in the background, suggesting proximity to San Salvador. The segment ends with a news montage showing armed soldiers in tactical gear during a state of emergency, with on-screen text indicating 'El Salvador records deadliest day in 30 years', and Arabic subtitles translating the context. The final shot is a view from a moving vehicle approaching a high-security prison wall with a watchtower, reinforcing the theme of surveillance and control.

discovered_new_entities.CECOT prison: A large, modern prison complex with high concrete walls, barbed wire, and signage reading 'CECOT - Centro de Confinamiento del Terrorismo', shown from aerial and ground-level perspectives, emphasizing its role in detaining individuals linked to terrorism or gang activity.

segments.caption: The video opens with an aerial view of the CECOT prison, a large, modern facility with high concrete walls, barbed wire, and a watchtower, set against a backdrop of green hills and farmland. The camera then moves to ground level, showing the entrance gate with the sign 'CECOT - Centro de Confinamiento del Terrorismo' and armed guards in camouflage uniforms standing at attention. A man in a red t-shirt with 'LES BELLAMINS B.S.P.H.C UNDERGROUND' printed on the back exits a van and walks toward the gate, gesturing as he speaks. He is then seen inside the prison, walking through a brightly lit corridor with other visitors, passing by a sign for 'PERSONAL ADMINISTRATIVO'. He enters a security checkpoint area with metal detectors and X-ray machines, where masked prison guards in blue uniforms monitor the process. The man continues walking through the facility, passing by a room labeled 'CONTROL DE ACCESO', while Arabic subtitles appear, translating his dialogue about the lack of a network in the area. The scene briefly cuts to a map of El Salvador highlighting the CECOT location, before returning to the man inside the prison, where he is greeted by a guard who identifies himself as the director of CECOT. The video ends with the man walking through the security checkpoint, placing his belongings on a table for inspection, as a camera operator films the scene.

active_entities.Man in red t-shirt: A man with a beard wearing a red t-shirt with 'LES BELLAMINS B.S.P.H.C UNDERGROUND' printed on the back, standing outside the CECOT prison, gesturing toward the facility, and later seen inside a van during a drive, engaging in conversation.

discovered_new_entities.Camera operator: A person holding a professional camera with a stabilizer, filming the man in the red t-shirt as he goes through the security checkpoint inside the CECOT prison.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Man in red t-shirt approaching a security checkpoint inside the CECOT prison, marked by a large 'Intercept' metal detector. He walks through the detector, which emits a red light, and is then greeted by a Masked prison guard in a light blue uniform who gestures for him to proceed. The man hands over his phone and ID at a metal table, where the guard scans them. After a brief interaction, the guard directs him forward, and the man walks through a large, sterile, brightly lit corridor with white and light-blue walls. He is then stopped by another Masked prison guard who conducts a physical pat-down, checking his arms, back, and legs, and inspects his white Nike sneakers. The man remains calm and cooperative throughout. After the search, he continues walking through the facility, passing by a sign reading 'CONTROL DE ACCESO 1' and another labeled 'SANITARIO'. He exits the building into a covered outdoor area with a paved ground, where he walks alongside a guard toward a door marked 'PRIVADOS DE LIBERTAD'. He enters the building again, passing a sign that reads 'OBLIGATORIO USO DE ALCOHOL GEL', and proceeds deeper into the facility. The environment is clean, institutional, and highly controlled, with fluorescent lighting and minimal decoration. The camera operator is visible in the background, filming the process with a stabilizer, capturing the man’s journey through the security protocol.

active_entities.Man in red t-shirt: A man with a beard wearing a red t-shirt with 'LES BELLAMINS B.S.P.H.C UNDERGROUND' printed on the back, undergoing a security screening process inside the CECOT prison, including walking through a metal detector, handing over his phone and ID, and being physically searched by guards.

segments.caption: The video opens with an aerial view of the CECOT prison, a large, modern facility with high concrete walls and multiple buildings, surrounded by greenery and a body of water, emphasizing its isolated and secure location. The scene transitions to the interior, where the Man in red t-shirt is seen walking through a security checkpoint, passing through a metal detector labeled 'Intercept' and handing over his phone and ID at a security table. He is then subjected to a physical pat-down by Masked prison guards, who are dressed in black uniforms with balaclavas, ensuring thorough screening. The camera operator, holding a professional camera with a stabilizer, films the process from behind, capturing the man’s movements and interactions. The man proceeds through a corridor lined with cells, where Tattooed inmates are seated on the floor, visibly marked with extensive tattoos, under the watchful eyes of guards. The environment is sterile and brightly lit, with signs in Spanish such as 'CONTROL DE ACCESO 1' and 'OBLIGATORIO USO DE ALCOHOL GEL' visible, reinforcing the strict protocols. The man then enters a large, empty room labeled 'AFILIACIÓN', where he gestures toward the space, possibly explaining its function. He walks through a hallway with a sign for 'SALIDA' and approaches a covered outdoor area, where a guard in a black uniform with 'EL SALVADOR' on the sleeve speaks to him, explaining the rapid construction of the CECOT prison in seven months and its necessity to control dangerous criminals. The guard gestures toward the facility, emphasizing its role in addressing societal pain and crime. The man listens attentively, standing near the entrance of the covered area, as the guard continues his explanation, with the CECOT logo visible on the gate in the background.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of the CECOT prison's single entrance, a long, covered driveway flanked by high walls and a metal roof, with signage indicating 'CECOT - Centro de Confinamiento del Terrorismo' and 'CONTROL DE ACCESO 1'. The scene transitions to a conversation between the Guard in black uniform and the Man in red t-shirt, who is asking about the prison's purpose. The guard gestures toward the facility as they walk, emphasizing its design. The camera then cuts to an aerial night view of the entire CECOT complex, illuminated with blue lights, showcasing its massive, grid-like structure. This is followed by a map of El Salvador, highlighting the country’s location in Central America, with animated markers for gangs 'MS-13' and 'Barrio 18' appearing over the map. The video then shows a chaotic scene of Tattooed inmates being subdued by Masked prison guards in a corridor, with one inmate running bare-chested. The Man in red t-shirt is later seen walking through the prison grounds, speaking to the camera, while the Guard in black uniform walks ahead. The segment ends with a close-up of a watchtower and barbed wire fencing, reinforcing the high-security environment. Throughout, Arabic and English subtitles provide context about the prison’s single entrance and exit, and the reasons for its creation.

segments.caption: The video segment follows the Man in red t-shirt as he is escorted by a Masked prison guard through the CECOT prison complex. They walk along a paved outdoor path under a clear blue sky, passing a high-security building with a watchtower and barbed wire, before entering a covered walkway. Inside, they proceed down a brightly lit corridor with blue-painted lower walls, passing other guards in black uniforms. The guard leads the man to a room marked 'ARMERÍA' (Armory) under the CECOT logo. Inside the armory, rows of rifles are displayed on metal racks, and the guard demonstrates a firearm while speaking to the man. The man gestures and asks questions, and the guard responds, confirming the weapon type. The scene includes close-ups of the rifles and a table with handcuffs and chains, emphasizing the armory's function. On-screen text in Arabic and English provides context, such as 'Now, we are going to the armory' and 'Here we are in the weapons warehouse.' The environment is sterile, well-lit, and highly organized, reflecting the prison's strict security protocols.

segments.caption: The video opens inside the weapons warehouse of the CECOT prison, where the Man in red t-shirt is being shown various armaments by a Masked prison guard. The guard, wearing a black uniform with 'EL SALVADOR' on the sleeve and a cap, gestures toward rows of rifles and explains their use, emphasizing control and security. The Man in red t-shirt listens intently, then picks up a set of handcuffs and chains, examining them closely. The guard demonstrates a large metal chain, and the man reacts with a surprised expression. The scene shifts to the guard holding a baton, labeled 'The forta' in Arabic subtitles, before transitioning to the man standing between two heavily armored officers in riot gear, each holding a transparent shield marked 'DGCP'. The man gestures animatedly, seemingly explaining or narrating the situation. The group then exits the armory, walking through a corridor and out into the prison yard. The camera follows them as they approach a vehicle, and the scene cuts to a first-person view from inside a car driving along a road toward the prison complex, passing a watchtower and barbed wire fencing. The drive continues past the prison’s exterior, showing its modern, high-security design with white and blue walls, before arriving at a covered area where the man is greeted by another guard. The environment is bright and sunlit, with clear skies and distant mountains visible, emphasizing the prison’s isolated and fortified nature.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with the Man in red t-shirt ascending a set of concrete stairs outside a modern, industrial-style building marked with a sign reading 'COMEDOR AGENTES' (Agents' Dining Hall). He is escorted by a Masked prison guard in a black uniform, who wears a cap with 'JUSTICIA' (Justice) on the back and a badge with 'CECOT' and 'EL SALVADOR' insignia. They enter a large, empty dining hall with rows of black tables and chairs, illuminated by bright overhead lights and ceiling fans. The guard gestures toward the space while speaking to the man, who responds with expressive hand movements and smiles, indicating a conversation about the facility. Arabic subtitles appear, translating the dialogue, including phrases like 'هل تشعر أنك والحراس هنا بالمسؤولية' (Do you feel responsible with the guards here?) and 'شكراً لكم على خدمتكم' (Thank you for your service). The man then walks through a covered outdoor corridor with a mirror and a wall-mounted fan, briefly checking his reflection before continuing. He proceeds down a long, sterile hallway with blue-painted lower walls, passing a fire extinguisher and a door labeled 'COCINA' (Kitchen), accompanied by the guard. They enter a dormitory filled with rows of metal bunk beds, where the man picks up a dumbbell from a rack, and the guard points toward the beds while speaking. Subtitles reveal the guard discussing the psychological impact of being in front of criminals. The man lifts the dumbbell, engaging in a light workout, while the guard continues to explain the environment. The setting remains consistently bright and institutional, emphasizing the clean, organized, and secure nature of the CECOT prison.

segments.caption: The video begins inside a well-lit gym with various exercise equipment, where the Man in red t-shirt is seen lifting dumbbells and then walking while gesturing, accompanied by a Masked prison guard in black uniform. They exit the building and walk along a sunlit outdoor path toward a high-security facility, passing a chain-link gate topped with razor wire. The guard leads the man through the gate, which is labeled 'ZONA 2 MÓDULOS 3-4 CECOT', indicating the Center of Confinement of Terrorism. As they proceed, the man points toward a large white building with a curved roof, and the camera pans to show guards on an upper balcony. The scene transitions to the man being escorted into a covered corridor with a sign reading 'MÓDULO 4 CECOT', then into a long, brightly lit prison hallway lined with barred cells. The hallway is marked with a sign 'CELDAS' above the entrance, and the man walks down the corridor, greeting guards with 'Good evening' as he passes. Other guards and inmates are visible in the background, some standing near cell doors, while the man continues walking, observing the environment. The atmosphere is structured and controlled, emphasizing the high-security nature of the CECOT prison.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a wide shot of a prison corridor lined with barred cells, where numerous tattooed inmates in white uniforms and face masks are standing or sitting inside, observing the surroundings. The camera then follows the Man in red t-shirt as he walks down the long, brightly lit corridor, flanked by Masked prison guards in black uniforms who stand at intervals along the hallway. The corridor features a curved ceiling with linear lighting, yellow floor markings, and multiple cells on both sides, each marked with numbers like '7' and '31'. As the man walks, he gestures and speaks, with Arabic subtitles appearing on screen describing the inmates' white uniforms, T-shirts, white shorts, and white clogs, emphasizing that these are the only possessions they have. Close-ups reveal the inmates' intense gazes and detailed tattoos on their heads and arms, some resting on metal surfaces inside their cells. The Man in red t-shirt continues walking, occasionally turning to look at the inmates, while guards maintain a vigilant presence. The atmosphere is tense and controlled, with the prison’s modern, high-security design evident in the clean, structured environment and the uniformed personnel overseeing the area.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a wide shot of a long, brightly lit prison corridor in the CECOT prison, featuring a curved ceiling with linear lighting and barred cells lining both sides. The Man in red t-shirt walks through the corridor, accompanied by a Masked prison guard in a black uniform who gestures and speaks to him, explaining the facility’s operations. The guard emphasizes 24/7 control and monitoring, with Arabic and English subtitles appearing on screen. The camera cuts to close-ups of Tattooed inmates inside the cells, many wearing blue surgical masks and displaying intricate head tattoos, some looking directly at the camera with intense expressions. The scene shifts back to the corridor, where the Man in red t-shirt continues his tour, gesturing as he listens. The camera then focuses on a group of Masked prison guards in gray uniforms and balaclavas conducting a security check on inmates inside a cell, using handcuffs and performing physical checks. The inmates, still in white uniforms and masks, sit or stand behind bars, some with visible tattoos on their arms and legs. The guards methodically secure the inmates, demonstrating the strict control within the facility. The environment remains sterile and highly structured, with yellow floor lines marking pathways and guards stationed at intervals. The overall atmosphere is tense and controlled, emphasizing the high-security nature of the prison.

segments.caption: The video segment unfolds in a brightly lit, modern prison corridor with a curved ceiling and rows of barred cells on both sides, marked by a sign 'CELDAS' above the entrance. Masked prison guards in gray uniforms and black balaclavas are seen conducting security checks on inmates. One guard, with visible tattoos on his arm, is actively handcuffing an inmate in a white uniform and blue surgical mask, who is being led out of a cell. The inmate raises his hands as he is restrained, and other inmates inside the cell, similarly dressed in white and wearing masks, watch from behind the bars. The guards maintain a vigilant presence, with one holding a baton. As the handcuffed inmate is escorted down the corridor, more guards and inmates are visible in the background, some standing in line or observing. The atmosphere is tense and controlled, emphasizing the strict security protocols. The corridor features yellow floor markings and a clean, industrial aesthetic, with bright overhead lighting illuminating the scene. The guards' movements are deliberate and coordinated, ensuring order as the inmate is moved away from the cell block.

active_entities.Masked prison guards: Uniformed officers in gray shirts and black balaclavas, conducting security checks, including handcuffing an inmate and maintaining vigilance with batons in hand, one displaying visible tattoos on his arm.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a dynamic, handheld tracking shot moving through a long, brightly lit prison corridor of the CECOT prison, characterized by its modern design with a curved ceiling and rows of barred cells on both sides. Masked prison guards in gray uniforms and black balaclavas escort a line of inmates in white uniforms and blue surgical masks, some with visible tattoos on their arms and heads. The guards maintain tight control, with some holding batons and others guiding inmates by the arms. The inmates are led in a single file along a yellow line on the floor, while other inmates observe from behind the bars of the cells. The camera follows closely, capturing the tense atmosphere and the disciplined movement. As the procession continues, the inmates are directed to sit on the floor in a semi-circle, maintaining a seated position with hands either on their knees or restrained. The camera zooms in on several tattooed inmates, highlighting intricate designs on their heads and necks, including one with 'MS-13' tattooed on his forehead. The environment remains sterile and controlled, with guards standing vigilantly around the seated group. The lighting is cool and clinical, emphasizing the institutional setting. The sequence ends with a wide shot showing the full group of seated inmates, surrounded by guards, with the corridor stretching into the distance, reinforcing the scale and security of the facility.

segments.caption: The video segment opens in a long, brightly lit prison corridor of the CECOT prison, with barred cells lining both sides and a high, curved ceiling with linear lighting. A group of inmates in white uniforms and blue surgical masks, many with visible tattoos on their arms and heads, are initially seen sitting in a semi-circle on the floor, facing a central figure who appears to be leading them. The leader, also in white with prominent head and arm tattoos, stands with hands clasped behind his back, addressing the group. Arabic subtitles appear, translating the spoken words: 'We are your people, we are your life, open the heavens.' The inmates then transition from sitting to standing in formation, performing synchronized movements—raising arms, pointing forward, and extending hands in unison—suggesting a choreographed or ritualistic act. Masked prison guards in gray uniforms and black balaclavas stand vigilantly along the corridor, some holding batons, observing the group. The camera captures close-ups of the inmates’ tattooed limbs and white Crocs, emphasizing their uniformity and the starkness of the environment. The atmosphere is tense yet disciplined, with the inmates’ movements precise and coordinated, while guards maintain a watchful presence. The setting remains consistent throughout, with the yellow floor line and cell numbers (e.g., 15, 16) visible, reinforcing the institutional structure of the CECOT prison.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of the prison corridor at CECOT, where a group of Tattooed inmates in white uniforms and blue surgical masks perform synchronized physical exercises under the watchful eyes of Masked prison guards in gray shirts and black balaclavas. The inmates, many with visible tattoos on their arms and heads, move in unison, bending and raising their arms in a choreographed routine. The corridor is long and brightly lit, with barred cells lining both sides and yellow floor markings. The scene transitions to a close-up of a medical staff member in blue scrubs and a hairnet, standing near a cell, as inmates observe from behind bars. The focus then shifts to the Man in red t-shirt, who is being escorted by a Guard in black uniform through the prison. He walks past the medical area, marked by a sign reading 'ÁREA MÉDICA', and enters a narrow, concrete-walled corridor. He is then shown in a small room with a large screen displaying a court interface, where the guard explains that inmates are seated here and can visualize the court and other linked prisoners through the screen. The man gestures toward the screen as the guard points to it, with Arabic subtitles translating the explanation. The scene ends with the man and guard walking back toward the main corridor, passing by the clinic and treatment area, as the guard continues to explain the facility’s functions.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a wide shot of a prison corridor, where a group of tattooed inmates in white uniforms are seated in a semi-circle on the floor, facing a central figure. The environment is stark, with high ceilings, barred cells lining the walls, and a yellow line marking the floor. The Man in red t-shirt, wearing a maroon shirt with 'LES BELLAMINS B.S.P.H.C UNDERGROUND' printed on the back, walks through the corridor, observing the inmates. He is accompanied by a camera operator and a woman holding a microphone, indicating a documentary-style recording. The Masked prison guards, dressed in gray uniforms and black balaclavas, stand vigilantly along the corridor, some holding batons. The Man in red t-shirt then engages in conversation with a Guard in black uniform, who gestures while speaking, possibly explaining the inmates' profiles. The camera focuses on a specific inmate, who is handcuffed and chained, with visible tattoos on his arms and torso. The Guard in black uniform holds a document, and subtitles reveal that the inmate is an active member of the gang 'Barrio 18' with a sentence of over 100 years. The inmate lifts his shirt to reveal more tattoos, including a large design on his side, while the guard continues to speak. The scene emphasizes the controlled, high-security environment of the CECOT prison, with the inmates' synchronized movements and the guards' strict oversight creating a tense, ritualistic atmosphere.

segments.caption: The video segment opens in a stark, brightly lit prison corridor with pale green walls, where several inmates in white uniforms are being processed by guards. The focus shifts to a tattooed inmate with intricate chest and arm tattoos, including a skull and a woman’s face, who lifts his shirt to reveal more tattoos while being observed by a guard in a black uniform holding a clipboard. The camera then pans to another heavily tattooed inmate with a goatee, whose chest displays names like 'Paula', 'Lorena', and 'Homie Lonely', along with dates and symbolic imagery, suggesting personal or gang-related significance. A man in a red t-shirt with 'LES BELLAMINS B.S.P.H.C UNDERGROUND' printed on the back, identified as the man in the red t-shirt, gestures and speaks animatedly to the guard, possibly explaining or narrating the scene. The setting includes a sign reading 'CELDA DE AISLAMIENTO' (Isolation Cell) and 'CECOT', indicating the location is within the CECOT prison. Other inmates, some with visible tattoos and handcuffs, are seen lifting their shirts or standing in line, while masked prison guards in gray uniforms and black balaclavas stand vigilantly with batons. One inmate, with a shaved head and a visible tattoo on his arm, is shown in close-up, looking down with a somber expression, as Arabic subtitles appear stating 'and later murdered her.' The scene transitions to a wider shot of inmates kneeling in a line in a large, open hall with barred windows, under the watchful eyes of guards in tactical gear, suggesting a ritualistic or disciplinary gathering. The atmosphere is tense and controlled, emphasizing the high-security environment and the physical and emotional weight of incarceration.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of heavily tattooed inmates sitting on the floor in a large, brightly lit prison corridor, their bodies covered in intricate designs including names, dates, and symbolic imagery like skulls and women’s faces. The camera pans across the group, revealing their shaved heads and white prison uniforms, while masked prison guards in gray shirts and black balaclavas stand vigilantly along the corridor, some holding batons. A man in a red t-shirt with 'LES BELLAMINS B.S.P.H.C UNDERGROUND' printed on the back, accompanied by a guard in black uniform, walks through the corridor, gesturing and engaging in conversation with the guard. The camera follows them as they approach the inmates, who remain seated and still. The man in the red t-shirt points to specific tattoos on one inmate’s back, prompting a close-up of the detailed artwork, including a tombstone with the name 'Santana Guzmán' and the phrase 'R.I.P.' The subtitles reveal that these tattoos are worn in memory of deceased gang members or family, with one inmate identifying a tattoo as his mother’s name. The guard in black uniform, holding a clipboard, inspects the tattoos and interacts with the inmates, while the camera captures the somber atmosphere of the prison. The setting is a modern, high-security facility with barred cells lining the corridor, marked by signs like '19' and '13', and a yellow line on the floor demarcating boundaries. The lighting is stark and clinical, emphasizing the controlled and tense environment. The sequence ends with the man in the red t-shirt continuing his conversation with the guard, while the inmates remain seated, their expressions serious and reflective.

segments.caption: The video segment opens in a brightly lit, high-security prison corridor at CECOT, where a group of tattooed inmates in white uniforms are seated on the floor in an orderly line. The Man in red t-shirt, accompanied by a Guard in black uniform, walks through the corridor, gesturing and conversing as he observes the inmates. The Guard in black uniform points at specific tattoos on an inmate’s head and back, with Arabic subtitles indicating the presence of women’s faces, skulls, and marijuana imagery in the designs. The camera pans across the inmates, revealing extensive tattoos covering their heads, chests, and arms, including names like 'Santana Guzmán' and symbolic imagery. The Man in red t-shirt continues to walk down the corridor, turning to speak to the camera, while masked prison guards in gray shirts and black balaclavas stand vigilantly along the sides. The scene shifts to a view through barred cell doors, showing more tattooed inmates standing in formation, some with shaved heads and others with light blue hair, all wearing white uniforms. The Man in red t-shirt gestures toward the cells, emphasizing the scale of the population. The camera captures close-ups of individual inmates, including one with a somber expression and Arabic subtitles translating to 'and later murdered her,' adding a narrative layer. The segment concludes with a wide shot of the corridor, showing the full line of seated inmates, the guards, and the structured, controlled environment of the prison, with the Man in red t-shirt continuing his tour and commentary.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Man in red t-shirt standing in front of a barred cell at CECOT prison, speaking into a microphone while gesturing toward Tattooed inmates behind the bars, many of whom are shirtless and display extensive tattoos on their bodies. The scene transitions to a wide shot of a prison corridor where the Man in red t-shirt walks down the hall, flanked by Masked prison guards, as Tattooed inmates sit cross-legged on the floor in a line, some with visible handcuffs and chains. He turns and gestures toward the camera, then proceeds down the corridor, passing by more guards and inmates. He is escorted by a Guard in black uniform toward a heavy metal door marked 'CECOT 5', which is opened to reveal a dark, bare cell with only a cement slab floor. The Man in red t-shirt enters, holding a flashlight, and gestures to the sparse interior, emphasizing the minimal conditions. He then exits and walks down another corridor, passing a cell with a yellow bucket and a sink. The scene shifts to a dining area where a worker in a hairnet pushes a cart of stacked plastic containers filled with food, handing them through the bars to inmates. Close-ups show the food being distributed—rice, beans, and bread—into individual containers, with inmates reaching through the bars to receive their meals. The environment is stark, with cold lighting and a clinical atmosphere, emphasizing the institutional nature of the prison.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a prison corridor where a worker in a hairnet and gloves pushes a food distribution cart filled with plastic containers of rice, beans, and bread, handing meals to tattooed inmates through bars. The inmates, dressed in white uniforms and wearing face masks, sit in a line, some with visible tattoos on their arms and chests, including one with 'Santana Guzmán' and another with a skull and woman’s face. The camera then cuts to an aerial view of the CECOT prison complex at dusk, showcasing its sprawling layout with high walls, watchtowers, and a central entrance. The scene shifts to a stark, white-walled cell where the Man in red t-shirt, flanked by two Masked prison guards in full riot gear, interviews a seated inmate. The inmate, also in a white uniform and mask, is handcuffed and has a large tattoo on his chest. The Man in red t-shirt gestures while asking questions, and the inmate lifts his shirt to reveal more tattoos, including a large 'MS' design. Subtitles in Arabic appear, translating questions like 'Do you regret what you did?' and 'How do you feel about your punishment?'. The inmate responds with a somber expression, and the guards remain vigilant, holding batons. The scene briefly cuts to a wider corridor view, showing other inmates being escorted by guards, before returning to the interview. The Man in red t-shirt continues questioning, and the inmate reveals more of his chest tattoo, emphasizing the gravity of his past actions. The guards stand silently, maintaining control, as the interview concludes with the inmate looking down, reflecting on his situation.

segments.caption: The video opens with a tense scene inside a stark, dimly lit prison cell where a man in a red t-shirt, identified as the Man in red t-shirt, stands facing a seated inmate in white prison garb, flanked by two Masked prison guards in full riot gear. The inmate, with a shaved head and somber expression, is shown in a close-up with Arabic subtitles asking, 'How old were you when you joined the gang?' and the response '11 years old' appearing on screen. The Man in red t-shirt gestures while speaking, seemingly conducting an interview or interrogation. The scene cuts to an aerial view of the CECOT prison complex, a modern, high-security facility with white and blue walls, as the Man in red t-shirt walks through its sunlit corridors, arms outstretched, with a guard in black uniform standing at a doorway. The video then transitions to a dramatic political montage: a map of El Salvador is highlighted in 2019 with a portrait of Nayib Bukele and text showing '53.1% of the vote', followed by a nighttime scene of Bukele speaking from a balcony of the Palacio Nacional, with Arabic subtitles stating 'Today, El Salvador has broken all records.' The map reappears in 2024 with '64.65% of the vote', and Bukele declares El Salvador the 'safest country in the entire Western Hemisphere' as confetti falls. The video ends with a shot of children smiling in a sunny park, then cuts back to the Man in red t-shirt walking through the CECOT prison corridor, approaching a guard at a doorway, with the sun setting behind him, casting a golden glow over the landscape beyond the prison walls.

segments.caption: The video segment presents a serene and expansive aerial view of a rolling landscape at sunset. The sky is painted in deep blues and purples with streaks of orange and pink near the horizon, suggesting the sun has recently set or is setting. Below, a vast expanse of green fields and scattered trees stretches toward distant mountains, which appear as dark silhouettes against the twilight sky. The terrain is gently undulating, with patches of dense vegetation and open grasslands. The lighting is soft and golden, casting long shadows and highlighting the textures of the land. There are no visible human figures, structures, or movement, creating a tranquil and untouched natural atmosphere. This scene contrasts sharply with the previous prison and urban sequences, offering a moment of peaceful isolation and natural beauty.

active_entities.Sunset landscape: A wide, scenic view of rolling hills and mountains at sunset, with golden light illuminating the horizon and deep blue-purple clouds above, creating a tranquil and expansive natural vista.

entity_library.Man in red t-shirt: A man with a beard wearing a red t-shirt with 'LES BELLAMINS B.S.P.H.C UNDERGROUND' printed on the back, being escorted through the CECOT prison corridor, gesturing and engaging in conversation with the guard, pointing at tattoos on inmates.
entity_library.Tattooed inmates: Inmates in white uniforms, many with extensive tattoos on their chests, arms, and heads, including names like 'Santana Guzmán', dates, and symbolic imagery like skulls and women’s faces, sitting on the floor in a line, some lifting their shirts to reveal tattoos, with handcuffs and chains visible on their wrists and ankles.
entity_library.Masked prison guards: Uniformed officers in gray shirts and black balaclavas, standing along the corridor with batons, observing the inmates’ movements, maintaining a vigilant and controlled presence throughout the sequence.
entity_library.Nayib Bukele: The President of El Salvador, shown in formal attire during a public address, speaking at a podium with the Salvadoran flag behind him, with Arabic subtitles translating his statements about guaranteeing the safety of the people.
entity_library.El Salvador cityscape: An aerial view of a densely built city with greenery and a prominent volcano in the background, likely San Salvador, shown during a drive through the region.
entity_library.News montage: A compilation of news clips showing armed soldiers in tactical gear during a state of emergency, with on-screen text from NBC News Now stating 'El Salvador records deadliest day in 30 years', and Arabic subtitles translating the context.
entity_library.Soldiers in formation: Uniformed military personnel in helmets and tactical gear standing in rows during a public address by Bukele.
entity_library.Prison exterior at night: A dimly lit prison yard with high walls, barbed wire, and a watchtower, shown during a vehicle approach, reinforcing the high-security environment.
entity_library.CECOT prison: A modern, high-security prison complex with white and blue walls, signage reading 'ARMERÍA' and 'CECOT', featuring well-lit corridors and a weapons warehouse with organized racks of rifles.
entity_library.Camera operator: A person holding a professional camera with a stabilizer, filming the man in the red t-shirt as he goes through the security checkpoint inside the CECOT prison, visible in the background during the process.
entity_library.Metal detector: A large white and blue 'Intercept' metal detector with a red light strip, branded with 'Tek84 San Diego, CA USA', used to screen individuals entering the CECOT prison.
entity_library.Security table: A metal table with a bottle of hand sanitizer and a phone scanner, where the man hands over his belongings for inspection by the guard.
entity_library.Signage: Spanish signs including 'ÁREA MÉDICA' above the medical area entrance and 'COCINA' on a door in the hallway, indicating facility functions and restricted zones.
entity_library.Guard in black uniform: A prison guard in a black uniform with 'EL SALVADOR' on the sleeve, holding a clipboard and interacting with inmates, inspecting their tattoos and overseeing the processing procedure, speaking to the man in the red t-shirt.
entity_library.Aerial night view of CECOT: An illuminated, grid-like aerial view of the CECOT prison complex at night, showcasing its extensive layout and high-security design with blue lighting.
entity_library.Map of El Salvador: A digital map highlighting El Salvador in Central America, with animated markers for gangs 'MS-13' and 'Barrio 18' appearing over the country, indicating gang territories.
entity_library.Watchtower and barbed wire: A close-up of a concrete watchtower with a guard on the balcony, surrounded by multiple layers of barbed wire fencing, emphasizing the prison's security measures.
entity_library.Masked prison guard: A uniformed officer in black attire, wearing a cap and sunglasses, escorting the man through the prison and demonstrating firearms in the armory, speaking to the man and handling weapons.
entity_library.Rifles: Multiple black rifles displayed on metal racks in the armory, with close-ups showing their design and handling by the guard, indicating standard issue weapons for the facility.
entity_library.Handcuffs and chains: Metal handcuffs and chains securing the wrists and ankles of an inmate, visible during the close-up as he lifts his shirt to reveal tattoos, emphasizing the physical restraints used in the prison.
entity_library.Weapons warehouse: A secure, well-lit room within the CECOT prison containing organized racks of rifles and other restraints, marked by signage as 'ARMERÍA', serving as the storage and distribution point for prison armaments.
entity_library.Dining hall: A large, empty room with rows of black tables and chairs, white walls, ceiling fans, and bright fluorescent lighting, serving as the agents' dining area within the CECOT prison.
entity_library.Dormitory: A spacious room with multiple metal bunk beds, white mattresses, and a weight rack with dumbbells, indicating a living and exercise area for prison staff or trainees.
entity_library.Wall-mounted fan: A black oscillating fan mounted on a wooden bracket on a white wall in a covered corridor, providing ventilation in the facility.
entity_library.Prison corridor: A long, brightly lit hallway with pale green walls, marked by a sign reading 'CELDA DE AISLAMIENTO' and 'CECOT', lined with barred cells and featuring a metal door, serving as the primary setting for the inmates’ inspection and processing.
entity_library.Medical staff member: A person in blue scrubs and a hairnet, standing near a cell, interacting with inmates and overseeing medical procedures.
entity_library.Court visualization room: A small, plain-walled room with a large screen displaying a court interface, where inmates are seated and can view the court and other linked prisoners, with a guard explaining its function to the man in the red t-shirt.
entity_library.Screen interface: A large wall-mounted screen displaying a digital court interface with options like 'Welcome', 'Start new hearing', and 'Select your hearing', used for remote court visualization and inmate communication.
entity_library.Arabic subtitles: On-screen text in Arabic, appearing during the close-up of a somber inmate, translating to 'Do you regret what you did?' and 'How do you feel about your punishment?', adding a narrative layer to the visual content and suggesting a backstory of violence or crime.
entity_library.Cell interior: A dark, bare cell with concrete walls and floor, containing only a cement slab, a sink, and a yellow bucket, illuminated by a flashlight held by the Man in red t-shirt.
entity_library.Food distribution cart: A metal cart stacked with clear plastic containers filled with rice, beans, and bread, being pushed by a worker in a hairnet and gloves, used to distribute meals to inmates through the bars.
entity_library.Food container: Clear plastic containers with lids, stacked on a cart, filled with layers of rice, beans, and bread, used for meal distribution to inmates.
entity_library.Worker in hairnet: A person wearing a white hairnet, gloves, and a white shirt, pushing the food cart and handing out meals to inmates through the bars.
entity_library.Inmate in white uniform: A seated inmate in a white prison uniform with a shaved head, appearing somber and restrained, with handcuffs and chains visible on his wrists and ankles, responding to questions about joining a gang at age 11.
entity_library.Palacio Nacional: A grand, illuminated neoclassical building with columns and a balcony, where Nayib Bukele delivers a speech to a crowd, with confetti falling as he declares El Salvador the safest country in the Western Hemisphere.
entity_library.Children in park: Three children, two girls and one boy, smiling at the camera in a sunny park with blooming trees and greenery in the background, symbolizing safety and normalcy.
entity_library.Sunset landscape: A wide, scenic view of rolling hills and mountains at sunset, with golden light illuminating the horizon and deep blue-purple clouds above, creating a tranquil and expansive natural vista.
